name: first-class
description: Christopher W. Shaw's First Class — an urgent history and defense of the U.S. Postal Service against the corporate and political forces pushing privatization.
useCase: Understanding the USPS crisis, privatization threat, postal banking, democracy and mail, rural communities, fighting back.
knownRisks: The book is a political argument — present both sides fairly
Postal service is a complex policy area with genuine trade-offs
Some information may date from 2021 — check for updates on USPS reform
